other_observers: Nathan Pieplow and many others; not sure
specific_location: private residence adjacent to the lower part of Gennesee Mountain Park
description: the bird was a large oriole, with a medium length thin and pointed bill that had a slight decurve. The bill was a dark grayish blue, as were the legs.  The head, back, and chest were all black, forming a black "cowl". The remainder of the underparts were yellow, as were the uppertail coverts and rump. The bird had a yellow shoulder patch, and two bold white wingbars on otherwise black wings. The tail was black, with the upper part of the outer tail feathers being yellow.Behaviors: The bird was coming to a hummingbird feeder in the ponderosa forest.Call: none heardPlumage: adult alternate
